# Codify "PR Diff === PR Body" mental model in AGENTS.md

### Context
Following the merge of PR #11530 and the recent Ideation Sandbox discussion, Tobias requested that we explicitly codify the "PR Diff === PR Body" mental model directly in `AGENTS.md`. This rule treats the PR body as carrying an equal substrate load to the diff itself.

### The Problem
Agents may default to the standard industry pattern where the code diff is the primary source of truth, and the PR body is just summary or administrative glue. In Neo.mjs, the PR body and review templates are graph-ingestion substrate (Native Edge Graph + DreamService) and memory anchors (per Discussion #11376). When this is not codified in `AGENTS.md`, agents may skip or under-fill the PR templates.

### The Architectural Reality
`AGENTS.md` is the fundamental per-turn execution anchor for all agents. Space in `AGENTS.md` is at a premium (hard cap of 24KB). We need to inject this critical rule while actively defending against substrate bloat.

### The Fix
1. Add a new 1-liner section `## 13.3. PR Diff === PR Body (Memory Anchor)` inside `AGENTS.md`.
2. Compress `## 13.1.` to offset the exact byte count added by `13.3`, preserving the total file size below the 24KB limit.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` contains the "PR Diff === PR Body" one-liner in section 13.
- [ ] Section 13.1 is compressed to net-neutralize the byte increase.
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` file size remains under the 24KB linter hard cap.

### Out of Scope
- Major restructuring of `AGENTS.md`.
- Changes to other `.agents/skills` files.

### Origin Session ID
Origin Session ID: d1aee218-8c42-4562-b2ec-f597284fa9d7

Replaces 5 full-URL markdown link patterns with their canonical short-form. GitHub's UI auto-resolves `#NNNNN` / `Discussion #NNNNN` within the canonical `neomjs/neo` repository context — the full `https://github.com/...` prefix is redundant overhead for cross-references that stay within the host repo.

Byte-budget impact (AGENTS.md is turn-loaded Map-tier; every byte matters):
- Pre: 24,501 bytes (75 under 24,576-byte hard cap; tight)
- Post: 24,247 bytes (329 under cap; comfortable headroom)
- Savings: 254 bytes

Affected sections (all are `<neo_core_overrides>` blocks where every byte matters most):
- §13.2 Friction → Gold (line 85): `[Discussion #10137](...)` → `Discussion #10137`
- §13.1 Contributions Over Commits (line 98): `[#11028](...)` → `#11028`
- §15.5 Neo Identity Anchor (line 112): 2 link normalizations (`#10119` + `#10137`)
- §15.6 Swarm Topology Anchor (line 134): `[Discussion #11026](...)` → `Discussion #11026`

Pure byte-format normalization. Zero rationale-prose stripping. No touching unrelated sections. No semantic content change. The visible text (the human/agent-readable `Discussion #NNNNN` prose) is fully preserved; only the wrapping `[…](url)` syntax is removed.

Unblocks:
- PR #11534 (#11533 graduation): intent-bearing §13.3 inline-addition now fits cleanly within budget; strict-scope §13.3-only PR can return without scope-violating compressions of unrelated sections
- PR #11450 (#10295 graduation): 84-addition security substrate gains byte-budget headroom

Substrate-discipline framing: per AGENTS.md §13 Substrate Accretion Defense ("Every substrate-mutation PR MUST EITHER net-reduce loaded-bytes OR cite future-decay-mitigation rationale"), this PR is a model citizen — net-reduces loaded bytes by 254 with zero behavioral change. Empirical anchor for the substrate-budget-pull anti-pattern (PR #11534 cycles 1-8): when byte cap forces rationale compression of unrelated content, the substrate-correct alternative is mechanical link-format normalization in scope-owned-budget-PR.

("For us: PR Diff === PR Body") — same L1 firewall pattern as §15.5
(Neo Identity Anchor) + §15.6 (Swarm Topology Anchor). Per operator
substrate-discipline insight: without naming the prior, future LLMs
reading AGENTS.md interpret "PR Diff === PR Body" as rhetorical
equation rather than training-prior override. Pattern empirically
anchored across 10-cycle convergence on this PR — byte-budget
optimization must NOT collapse the L1 firewall pattern.
